summary:
During the β-oxidation process for both even-and odd-length chain fatty acids, multiple molecules such as FADH, NADH are produced; however specifically concerning odd length chains, after removing an initial acetyl-CoA unit through unique processing at the end-stage, only then does it yield either two additional acetyl-CoAs like usual but also uniquely generates one instance of propionyl-CoA per full round of this specialized metabolic pathway leading directly into further metabolism via the citric acid cycle or other pathways involving methylmalonyl-CoA mutase activity converting said product appropriately within cellular biochemistry systems.
The summary should be concise while capturing all essential information related to how odd-chain fatty acids undergo β-oxidation producing specific metabolites including focusing particularly upon generation mechanisms resulting exclusively applicable solely when dealing strictly speaking about these particular types rather than general cases where standard rules apply equally across different lengths/families/types etc...
To summarize concisely yet comprehensively based purely off given context & requirements : 
During β-oxidation ,odd carbon number fatty acids result post-processing in formation of PropionylCoa alongwith Acetlycoas unlike evens which don't have special terminal step generating extra non-acetylcobalamin derivative . Key point being exclusive nature here due direct consequence mechanism acting differently ending up creating distinct intermediate crucial downstream utilization steps .
In essence Odd Chain Beta Oxidation yields distinctive Metabolite Propionic Acid Intermediate Not seen In Even Chains Due To Unique Processing At End Stage Resulting Direct Conversion Into Further Utilizable Forms Within Cell Biochemical Pathways Such As Citrate Cycle Or Methyl Malonate Mutations Converting It Appropriately For Cellular Energy Production And Other Purposes Beyond Simple Breakdown Steps Alone .
